Hearing Re: Defendants David Houriga and Backhoe Dispatch 24-7 Inc.'s Motion To Strike Plaintiff's Expert Witness.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants' motion to strike plaintiff's expert witness is denied. The Court largely adopt the judge pro tem's report and recommendations. Plaintiff does not appear to have engaged in purposeful gamesmanship. Defendants would suffer mild prejudice, while plaintiff would lose its expert in a key field. (See Du-All Safety, LLC v. Sup. Ct. (2019) 34 Cal.App.5th 485.) No sanctions are awarded.

Order signed.

HEARING RE: INTERVENOR VICTORIA FIRE & CASUALTY COMPANY's Motion For Leave To File Complaint In Intervention.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Victoria Fire & Casualty Company's motion for leave to file complaint in intervention is granted, with the corrections acknowledged in its reply brief that it is seeking to file an answer in intervention rather than a complaint and to defend the action in its own name. (Code Civ. Proc. sec. 387(b)(2).) Defendant Backhoe Dispatch 24-7, Inc. is a suspended corporation, and therefore is barred from defending itself in this action. An insurer is entitled to intervene in order to protect its own interests and those of its insured suspended corporation. (See Kaufman & Broad Communities, Inc. v. Performance Plastering, Inc. (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 212, 217-221.)

Order signed.
